09 Scorecard Based Leadership - YouTube.mp4
Believe it or not, once your team and company scorecards are in place, leadership becomes a fairly simple process. Said another way, your job becomes fairly simple. So what is your job? Simply put, your job is to help execute projects that turn red to yellow, and yellow to green. That's it. Assuming you and your team have selected the right KPIs, if you can keep your team focused on only those projects that turn red metrics into yellow and yellow metrics into green, your company will scale. It's not a matter of if, it's simply a matter of when. So said another way, what I want you to do is: I want you to turn green. Into yellow now. I realize that seems the reverse of what I just said, but I want you to raise the standards.
I want you to raise the targets such that what was previously green, you raise the targets and now that green score becomes yellow. This is when real scale begins to happen. Now, when you do this, make sure that you celebrate it, right? When you take a green metric that your team has just been hitting and you say, 'Hey, time to raise the standards,' I want you to celebrate that with your team. So yes, we celebrate. We celebrate when we turn red to yellow and we celebrate when we turn yellow to green, but we also celebrate when we turn green into yellow because that is when companies really start to scale. And again, it's not a matter of if. It is absolutely going to happen.
We'll talk more about when and how you'll execute these color-changing projects in a future module, but just know that if your algorithms are documented and your scorecards are functioning, scaling your business becomes very, very simple. Now note that I didn't say easy. I said simple. Okay. If building a business were easy, everyone would do it, but your life is about to get much, much simpler. You follow the steps I've just given you. Now, there's one last thing that I want you to know before we close out this session, and it's maybe the most important lesson of all. So please. Please, please pay attention. It is not your job to know how to turn red to yellow and yellow to green. I'm going to say that again because this is so.
So important. It is not your job to know how to turn red to yellow and yellow to green. If you have a team and if you are compensating them fairly, then you have every reason to expect that they will know how to close the gaps between where their metrics are today and the target goals that they have set for themselves. Yes, you should help them by providing your own ideas, by tapping your network, by freeing up additional budget and cash flow, but helping them does not make it your responsibility. In my experience, this is one of the hardest things for entrepreneurs to learn. But it is absolutely essential if you want to build a business that can scale without you.
Yes, having the right answers got you where you are today, but in the future, it will be asking the right questions to the right people that will get you where you want to go tomorrow. And it's these scorecards that will equip you with the knowledge and insights that you need to ask the right questions at the right time. To the right people. So, if you have your company scorecard in place, then congratulations. You are officially an instrument rated entrepreneur and your business is now able to scale without you needing to have all the answers. How cool is that?
